narrative: OncoHealth is a digital oncology management company — prior authorization / utilization management (OneUM), oncology real-world-data analytics (Oncology Insights), and the Iris virtual supportive-care platform (24/7 oncology nurses, mental-health therapists, dietitians, peer mentors) — serving 12M+ lives for health plans and employers. It holds the most sensitive data class there is: a named patient + cancer diagnosis + genomic markers + drug regimen + mental-health state, governed by HIPAA, GINA, and psychotherapy-note rules. The demo spine: PHI-rich prior-auth packets and Iris clinical/peer-mentor free-text coordination leaking, with a sharp precision story (their code-dense clinical criteria library, where context-aware PHI detection stays quiet but naive code-matching over-fires) and honest coverage gaps (genomic markers, MRNs, member IDs) plus an analytics re-identification risk.

findings.finding: Digital oncology management for health plans, employers, providers, and patients: utilization management / prior auth, oncology analytics, and telehealth supportive care. Supports 12M+ individuals across the US and Puerto Rico.

OneUM is an oncology-specific utilization management solution that streamlines prior authorizations for evidence-based care, including radiation oncology therapy (expansion noted by payers like Geisinger).

Iris by OncoHealth is a 24/7 virtual supportive-care service: oncology-certified nurses, oncology social workers, psychologists, dietitians, physician assistants, board-certified oncologists, and peer mentors, delivered via mobile/telehealth.
Oncology Insights is an analytics software solution leveraging real-world data to understand oncology drug spending and manage rising treatment costs; partners include life-science researchers.
PE-backed by Arsenal Capital Partners with strategic investment from McKesson Corporation; formerly Oncology Analytics (rebrand); significant revenue/membership/employee growth.

Core data is oncology PHI: cancer diagnoses (ICD-10 C/D codes), genomic/biomarker results (BRCA, EGFR, HER2, PD-L1), drug regimens, plus mental-health notes from Iris supportive care — implicating HIPAA, GINA, and psychotherapy-note protections.
findings.source: inference: oncology UM + telehealth + analytics business

findings.finding: Nightfall PROTECTED_HEALTH_INFORMATION fires on patient prose combos (name+DOB+'breast cancer'+ICD10+drug+'anxiety') but stays quiet on a criteria sentence with the same codes and no patient; ICD10_CODE fires on C-codes; FDA_NATIONAL_DRUG_NAME fires on Keytruda/pembrolizumab/trastuzumab; ICD10_DIAGNOSIS_DESCRIPTION and US_HEALTHCARE_NPI were unreliable on bare inputs.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prior-auth clinical packets are the crown-jewel PHI leak — identity + cancer Dx + genomic + regimen + narrative crossing org boundaries.
hypotheses.basis: OneUM streamlines oncology prior auth between practices, OncoHealth, and payers (finding: OneUM).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Iris care-team and peer-mentor coordination leaks cancer + mental-health PHI in free text.
hypotheses.basis: Iris defining workforce of nurses/social workers/psychologists/peer mentors coordinating 24/7 (finding: Iris telehealth).

hypotheses.hypothesis: The clinical criteria library is a false-positive minefield — code-dense by design; needs context-aware PHI detection, not code matching.
hypotheses.basis: PA criteria/guidelines are full of ICD-10 + drug names as work product; probe shows PHI detector stays quiet while ICD10_CODE over-fires.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Genomic/biomarker data (GINA) plus MRN and member IDs are protected but uncovered by off-the-shelf detectors.
hypotheses.basis: Biomarkers drive oncology decisions; data_type_map has mrn/insurance_member_id as null and no genomic detector.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Oncology Insights de-identified research exports risk re-identification when a residual identifier is left in.
hypotheses.basis: Real-world-data analytics shared with payers/researchers under BAAs (finding: Oncology Insights).

decisions.choice: Lead with PA-packet PHI + Iris free-text PHI, anchored by the context-aware-PHI-vs-naive-code precision story.
decisions.rationale: These are uniquely OncoHealth and existential under HIPAA/GINA; the criteria-library over-fire is their specific alert-fatigue pain.
decisions.drove: prior_auth_request.eml and iris_care_team_slack.txt as leads; clinical_pa_criteria.txt as the precision contrast.

decisions.choice: PHI-led (cancer Dx, genomic, mental health), then secrets + HR PII; no PCI.
decisions.rationale: They're B2B oncology management — padding with card data would be irrelevant (taxonomy: relevance over coverage).

decisions.choice: Real oncology ICD-10 C-codes, real drug names, genomic markers, PHI free-text combos; map phi_note->PROTECTED_HEALTH_INFORMATION, drug_name->FDA_NATIONAL_DRUG_NAME; leave genomic_marker/mrn/member_id as not_integrated.
decisions.rationale: Probe showed these fire reliably and the gaps are real, honest talking points (genetic data uncovered).
decisions.drove: local oncology_icd10/drug/genomic helpers; detectors.json data_type_map additions.

decisions.choice: Criteria-library ICD-10/drugs (naive over-fire vs PHI-quiet), member/claim # shaped like SSN, study-subject ID, placeholder key.
decisions.rationale: The criteria over-fire is the most resonant precision story; balanced with clean correctly-quiet wins.
decisions.drove: clinical_pa_criteria.txt scanned with both ICD10_CODE (fires) and PHI (quiet); internal_id_like_ssn; placeholder_key.

decisions.choice: PHI in clinician/peer-mentor prose, genomic results in prose, spaced MRN/SSN, Dx split across sentences.
decisions.rationale: Recall story grounded in how oncology PHI actually appears in care coordination.
decisions.drove: fn_evasive plants in the PA packet, Iris Slack, roster, and research export.

decisions.choice: Iris oncology nurse as protagonist; medical director, external submitting oncologist, oncology social worker, peer mentor, data scientist, platform engineer, HR.
decisions.rationale: The defining clinical workforce sits exactly where cancer + mental-health PHI escapes into prose.
decisions.drove: consistent fabricated cast + fabricated patients across carriers.

decisions.choice: Full POC (7 carriers, ~32 plants); push to GitHub aman313-nf/demogorGenArtifacts under sessions/oncohealth/.
decisions.rationale: User confirmed full POC and GitHub persistence (same as the Fictiv session).

open_questions: Slack vs Microsoft Teams for the Iris care team — assumed Slack; swap surface if Teams.
Whether Iris's consumer side bills copays (would add a small PCI layer) — omitted to keep PHI focus; revisit if requested.
Exact payer-integration format (X12 270/271/278 EDI vs CSV) — modeled the roster as CSV for readability; could add an 837/278 EDI carrier later.
